title: Lucerne, Interlaken, Montreux & Zurich.

trains.name: Trains & Taxi Transfers
trains.blurb: Scheduled Swiss rail travel between cities using an 8-day Swiss Travel Pass, with transfers at each end.

transport.1: Zurich Airport to Lucerne by scheduled train, covered by the 8-day Swiss Travel Pass.
transport.2: Mount Titlis cable car included with the Swiss Travel Pass; Lake Lucerne cruise also included free with the pass.
transport.3: Lucerne to Interlaken by scheduled train, covered by the Swiss Travel Pass.
transport.5: Interlaken to Montreux by scheduled train, covered by the Swiss Travel Pass.
transport.7: Montreux to Zurich by scheduled train, covered by the Swiss Travel Pass.
transport.8: Zurich hotel to Zurich Airport by scheduled train, covered by the Swiss Travel Pass.

inclusions: 8-day consecutive Swiss Travel Pass, II Class, covering rail travel between all cities on the itinerary
Mount Titlis Rotair revolving cable car ticket
Lake Lucerne cruise, included free with the Swiss Travel Pass
All city-to-city train transfers as per itinerary
7 nights accommodation on a PI (half-board) basis

private.name: Private Exclusive Coach
private.blurb: A private coach with driver on disposal throughout, ideal for families and small groups wanting flexibility.

transport.1: Private coach transfer from Zurich Airport to Lucerne; private vehicle on disposal from Day 1 to Day 7.
transport.2: Private excursion to Titlis and Engelberg by private vehicle.
transport.3: Private coach transfer from Lucerne to Interlaken.
transport.5: Private coach transfer from Interlaken to Montreux.
transport.6: Private excursion to the Cailler chocolate factory in Broc, in the Gruyère region, including tasting tickets.
transport.7: Private coach transfer from Montreux to Zurich.
transport.8: Private transfer from the Zurich hotel to Zurich Airport.

inclusions: Private coach on disposal from Day 1 to Day 7
Private transfers Zurich Airport – Lucerne and Zurich hotel – Zurich Airport
Titlis and Engelberg excursion by private vehicle
Broc chocolate factory visit with tasting tickets
All private inter-city transfers as per itinerary
7 nights accommodation on a PI (half-board) basis

days.title: Arrive Zurich → Lucerne
days.desc: Arrive into Zurich and transfer south-west to Lucerne, set on the shores of Lake Lucerne beneath Mount Pilatus. Stroll across the wooden Chapel Bridge (Kapellbrücke) with its 17th-century painted gables, and take in the cobbled lanes and painted facades of the medieval Old Town.
days.experience: The Lion Monument, a dying lion carved into a sandstone cliff face to honour the Swiss Guards killed in 1792, is what Mark Twain called 'the most mournful and moving piece of rock in the world'.

days.title: Lucerne & Mount Titlis
days.desc: A full day in the high Alps: ride the world's first revolving Rotair cable car up to Mount Titlis at 3,020m, where a glacier cave and the Cliff Walk suspension bridge await above the snowline. Return to Lucerne for a scenic cruise on Lake Lucerne, framed by the surrounding peaks.
days.experience: The glacier cave carved into Titlis's permanent ice field, one of the most accessible in the Alps.

days.title: Lucerne → Interlaken
days.desc: Travel through the heart of Switzerland to Interlaken, the resort town wedged between Lake Thun and Lake Brienz beneath the Jungfrau massif. The afternoon is free to wander the flower-lined Höheweg promenade with its open views to the Eiger, Mönch and Jungfrau.
days.taste: Meringue, by local tradition invented in nearby Meiringen, is served everywhere in the Bernese Oberland piled high with thick double cream.

days.title: Interlaken at leisure
days.desc: A free day to explore the Bernese Oberland — take an optional excursion up to Jungfraujoch, the 'Top of Europe', or relax beside the turquoise waters of Lake Brienz and the postcard village of Iseltwald.
days.experience: Interlaken's central Höhematte park doubles as the valley's paragliding landing strip — all summer, canopies drift down over the town from launch points at Beatenberg and Schynige Platte.
days.fallbackIncluded: Day at leisure — optional Jungfraujoch excursion available.

days.title: Interlaken → Montreux
days.desc: Cross the Bernese Oberland to Montreux on the shores of Lake Geneva, famed for its mild micro-climate, palm-lined promenade and its Jazz Festival heritage. The medieval, moated Château de Chillon sits just along the lakeshore.
days.taste: The steep terraced vineyards of Lavaux, a UNESCO World Heritage landscape along this shore of Lake Geneva, produce Chasselas, the crisp local white poured in nearly every Montreux restaurant.

days.title: Montreux & Château de Chillon
days.desc: Spend the day around Lake Geneva: walk the lakeside promenade past the Freddie Mercury statue, and visit the Château de Chillon, one of Europe's most-visited castles. Travellers on the private itinerary continue into the Gruyère region to visit the Broc chocolate factory.
days.taste: Gruyère is Switzerland's heartland of chocolate and cheese-making — the Cailler factory at Broc, founded in 1819, is the country's oldest chocolatier.
days.experience: Château de Chillon, immortalised by Lord Byron's poem 'The Prisoner of Chillon'.

days.title: Montreux → Zurich
days.desc: Return north to Zurich, Switzerland's largest city, straddling the Limmat river at the edge of Lake Zurich. The Old Town's Grossmünster and Fraumünster churches, and the boutiques of Bahnhofstrasse, are all within easy walking distance of the hotels.
days.taste: Zürcher Geschnetzeltes — veal strips in a creamy mushroom sauce, served with rösti — is the city's signature dish, on the menu at old-world institutions like the Zeughauskeller, a former armoury turned beer hall.
days.shopping: Bahnhofstrasse is one of the world's most expensive shopping streets, lined with Swiss watch houses and department stores.

days.title: Depart Zurich
days.desc: Free time until your transfer to Zurich Airport for the onward flight, depending on flight schedule.

about.title: Best season
about.body: April–October for the best weather across the lakes and Alpine scenery; the Titlis excursion also runs through winter for snow.

about.title: Weather
about.body: Mild, changeable Alpine climate — warm summers around 18–25°C in the lowlands, cold and snowy at altitude and in winter, so pack layers.

about.title: Currency & docs
about.body: Switzerland's official currency is the Swiss Franc (CHF); Switzerland is in the Schengen area but not the EU, so standard Schengen entry rules apply.

goodToKnow.title: Tickets & timing
goodToKnow.body: The Titlis cable car and Château de Chillon both get busy in summer — travelling early in the day avoids the longest queues.

goodToKnow.title: Rail travel
goodToKnow.body: The Swiss Travel Pass covers trains, lake cruises and city transport included in this itinerary — keep it to hand for spot checks by conductors.

goodToKnow.title: Your vehicle
goodToKnow.body: Driver working hours are limited by Swiss law, so day-trip timings are planned around required rest breaks. Luggage space fits one large case per guest.

goodToKnow.title: What to pack
goodToKnow.body: Comfortable shoes for cobbled Old Towns, cable cars and lakeside walks, plus a layer for cooler evenings and higher altitudes.

goodToKnow.title: Money
goodToKnow.body: Cards are accepted almost everywhere in Switzerland, even for small purchases; a little cash is still useful for rural cafés and market stalls. Switzerland is consistently ranked among the most expensive countries in Europe, so budget accordingly.

terms: All rates are net and per person for the package.
Child rates apply for children aged 2 to 11 years old. One child sharing a room with 2 adults, subject to hotel policy.
All rates are subject to availability at the time of booking. Europe Incoming will endeavour to match the rates quoted.
Rates are not applicable during trade fair periods, major European public holidays, and other major events.
City taxes are not included in the price.
If certain attractions are closed during specific periods, alternative options will be arranged.
All bookings must be confirmed at least 60 working days prior to arrival.
100% pre-payment required by bank transfer or credit card (Visa or Mastercard). All bank transfer charges covered by the Agent.
Vouchers will be issued after receipt of full payment.
